articles.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
articles.summary: OpenAI launched a $100/month ChatGPT Pro subscription offering 5x more Codex usage than the $20 Plus tier, targeting power users and competing with Anthropic's Claude Max.

articles.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
articles.summary: Florida's Attorney General launched an investigation into OpenAI over public safety and national security concerns, citing risks of technology falling into foreign hands and ChatGPT's links to criminal behavior.

articles.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
articles.summary: Google's Gemini AI now generates interactive 3D models and simulations responding to user questions. Users can rotate models, adjust sliders, and modify values in real-time for dynamic simulations.

articles.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
articles.summary: OpenAI launched a $100/month ChatGPT Pro plan, filling the gap between the $20 and $200 monthly subscriptions for power users.

articles.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
articles.summary: Florida's Attorney General investigates OpenAI after ChatGPT allegedly helped plan a shooting at Florida State University that killed two and injured five. A victim's family plans to sue OpenAI.

articles.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
articles.summary: Following a data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor faces lawsuits and loses major customers, significantly impacting the company.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure that challenges AWS and Google Cloud by addressing their complexity and cost limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, while Goose offers identical open-source functionality free and locally. Goose eliminates subscription costs and cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment around Broadcom's acquisition. Customer dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware strategy is driving competitors to capitalize on market opportunities.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment plants, and refineries. Six US government agencies warn of the ongoing threat since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence (GRU) hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage. MikroTik and TP-Link devices were primarily targeted across 120 countries.

articles.title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
articles.summary: Synthetic turf installation in America surged from 7 million to 79 million square meters since 2001, raising environmental concerns about microplastics pollution despite industry claims of safety.

articles.title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
articles.summary: Cornell University installed synthetic turf on a former meadow for field hockey, replacing wildlife habitat with artificial grass. Environmental advocates are opposing the synthetic turf projects due to ecological concerns.

articles.title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
articles.summary: Desalination technology is increasingly vital for water-stressed regions, with Qatar producing 77% of fresh water through this method. The technology represents a crucial infrastructure, though vulnerabilities exist during geopolitical conflicts.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard GPUs. The advancement makes sophisticated virtual environment generation accessible without requiring expensive hardware.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers introduces multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling better understanding of text and images together. These models improve search and ranking across different content types.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve during task execution without retraining. The system allows continuous adaptation on-the-job for better performance.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps in artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and management.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available to advance sustainable farming practices and innovation.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new Gemini API inference tiers: Flex for cost-efficiency and Priority for lower latency, helping developers balance expenses with performance requirements.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map aimed at protecting the country's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, developing safety measures to protect people from harmful AI applications.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, with discussion of Mamba 3 and attention residuals in recent AI developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate new details in real time.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math/science visualizations.

articles.title: Agentic AI’s governance challenges under the EU AI Act in 2026
articles.summary: AI agents risk creating governance challenges as they operate without clear action records. EU AI Act enforcement in August 2026 will impose substantial penalties for organizations failing to trace and control agent decisions.

articles.title: Anthropic keeps new AI model private after it finds thousands of external vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview AI discovered thousands of cybersecurity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers. Rather than public release, the company shared it with over 40 organizations responsible for critical infrastructure through Project Glasswing.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI adoption is advancing rapidly in enterprise IT with 97% exploring agentic strategies, but governance and integration are lagging behind organizational capabilities, creating significant control risks.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from Penn State and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance unveiled Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and pathfinding challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets OpenClaw AI agents running on Mac Minis, exploiting macOS binaries to steal credentials as developers deploy local AI agents.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical AI limitations in real-world applications.

articles.title: Five AI Compute Architectures Every Engineer Should Know: CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs Compared
articles.summary: Modern AI relies on diverse specialized processors including CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs, each optimized for different workloads. Understanding these architectures is essential for engineers as enterprises shift toward workload-specific optimization.

articles.title: An End-to-End Coding Guide to NVIDIA KVPress for Long-Context LLM Inference, KV Cache Compression, and Memory-Efficient Generation
articles.summary: NVIDIA's KVPress optimizes long-context LLM inference through KV cache compression, reducing memory usage. The tutorial demonstrates practical implementation with experiments comparing compression strategies for efficient generation.

articles.title: Meta Superintelligence Lab Releases Muse Spark: A Multimodal Reasoning Model With Thought Compression and Parallel Agents
articles.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs unveiled Muse Spark, a natively multimodal reasoning model supporting tool-use, visual chain of thought, and multi-agent orchestration capabilities.

articles.title: Surprise: Not every Lenovo laptop is worth recommending in 2026 - the Yoga 7i is proof
articles.summary: Lenovo's Yoga 7i 2-in-1 is well-built but difficult to recommend due to mid-tier hardware that doesn't compete favorably with rival laptops.

articles.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared the OLED TVs and wasn't prepared for the upset
articles.summary: Samsung's 2026 S95H flagship OLED TV offers only modest upgrades over the previous S95F model, making the newer version not worth the upgrade for most consumers.

articles.title: Home Depot's new DeWalt deal comes with a free power tool - how to redeem the offer
articles.summary: Home Depot offers a DeWalt bundle featuring a string trimmer and leaf blower with a free choice of an additional tool or battery for lawn care projects.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article explores design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering AI agent architecture and implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers practical methods for evaluating agent performance and reliability in production environments.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, producing nonsensical results. Proper synchronization and resource locking are essential for reliable multi-agent orchestration systems.

articles.title: A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression
articles.summary: A comprehensive visual guide with over 100 visualizations explaining linear regression model building, quality measurement, and improvement techniques.

articles.title: How Visual-Language-Action (VLA) Models Work
articles.summary: VLA models combine visual, language, and action processing for humanoid robots. They use mathematical foundations to enable robots to understand and respond to visual and textual inputs.

articles.title: A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime
articles.summary: Guide to survival analysis using Python for customer retention modeling with Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a narrative game about grit and faith, is now available on GeForce NOW for cloud streaming across nearly any device.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs enabling robots to transform industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA collaborated to optimize Gemma 4, a small and efficient open model family for local agentic AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device intelligence with real-time context.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases improved foundation model versions. Understanding model lifecycle is crucial for planning AI applications and testing compatibility before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises manage agent sprawl by enabling discovery, sharing, and reuse of agents and tools across organizations while providing visibility and governance controls.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView component for React apps, enabling real-time visualization of AI agent browsing activities. Users can monitor and control autonomous web interactions with a three-line JSX integration.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source job scheduler managing 65% of TOP500 systems, integrates with Kubernetes for large-scale GPU workloads. Organizations leverage existing Slurm investments in scheduling policies and workflows for AI training.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA nvCOMP reduces LLM checkpoint costs using just 30 lines of Python. The technique compresses massive checkpoint files (782 GB for 70B models) to significantly decrease storage and training expenses.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: This article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, focusing on how proteins interact with each other to form complexes rather than functioning in isolation.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged their unique perspective to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability by analyzing how input features collectively drive model predictions beyond individual feature attribution.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach quantifies measurement quality through information estimation techniques.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
